school_objects.meaning: A bag with straps that you wear on your back to carry school supplies
school_objects.example: My blue backpack holds my lunch box, three books, pencils, and a water bottle.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A backpack is a bag you wear on your back to carry your school things.
school_objects.sentence: I pack my backpack every night before school.

school_objects.meaning: A long thin tool made of wood with a graphite center for writing and drawing
school_objects.example: I use my yellow pencil with an eraser on top to write my name on the paper.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A pencil is what you write with at school. You can erase mistakes!
school_objects.sentence: May I borrow a pencil, please?

school_objects.meaning: A writing tool that uses ink instead of graphite
school_objects.example: My teacher says we can use blue or black pens when we write our final drafts.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A pen writes with ink. You cannot erase pen, so be careful!
school_objects.sentence: I wrote my story with a blue pen.

school_objects.meaning: A small object that removes pencil marks from paper
school_objects.example: My pink eraser has a unicorn on it and smells like grapes when I use it.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: An eraser rubs away pencil mistakes so you can try again.
school_objects.sentence: I used my eraser to fix the wrong answer.

school_objects.meaning: A tool that makes dull pencils pointy again
school_objects.example: My pencil is dull and won't write well, so I put it in the sharpener.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A sharpener makes pencils pointy when they get dull from writing.
school_objects.sentence: I turn the handle of the sharpener to make my pencil pointy.

school_objects.meaning: A stick of colored wax used for drawing and coloring
school_objects.example: I use a red crayon to draw a heart for my mom on her birthday card.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Crayons are colorful sticks for drawing pictures. They come in 64 colors!
school_objects.sentence: I colored the sun yellow with my crayon.

school_objects.meaning: A pen with a thick tip and bright, colorful ink
school_objects.example: The purple marker is my favorite for coloring flowers and drawing rainbows.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Markers are bright pens for coloring. They have strong, vibrant colors.
school_objects.sentence: Don't forget to put the cap back on the marker.

school_objects.meaning: A pencil with colored wax inside instead of graphite
school_objects.example: I use colored pencils to draw detailed pictures because they don't smear like markers.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Colored pencils are like regular pencils but with colors inside.
school_objects.sentence: I need a green colored pencil for the grass.

school_objects.meaning: A straight tool for measuring length and drawing straight lines
school_objects.example: My ruler is 12 inches long and has a bumpy edge for cutting paper shapes.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A ruler measures how long something is in inches or centimeters.
school_objects.sentence: I used my ruler to draw a straight line.

school_objects.meaning: A tool with two blades and handles used for cutting paper and other materials
school_objects.example: I use scissors carefully to cut out a star shape from construction paper.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Scissors cut paper into shapes. Always be careful with the sharp blades!
school_objects.sentence: These scissors are for left-handed people.

school_objects.meaning: A sticky substance that makes things stick together
school_objects.example: I put white glue on the back of the picture and stick it to the construction paper.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Glue makes things stick. A little bit goes a long way!
school_objects.sentence: Wait for the glue to dry before you move your project.

school_objects.meaning: A solid stick of glue that twists up from a tube
school_objects.example: The purple glue stick goes on purple but dries clear, so I can see where I put it.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A glue stick is solid glue in a tube. Twist it up and rub it on paper.
school_objects.sentence: My glue stick is empty. I need a new one.

school_objects.meaning: A long thin strip with sticky adhesive on one or both sides
school_objects.example: I use clear tape to fix the torn page in my favorite book.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: Tape is sticky on one side and helps you stick things together.
school_objects.sentence: Cut a piece of tape and stick it over the tear.

school_objects.meaning: A book of blank or lined paper for writing notes and assignments
school_objects.example: My notebook has wide-ruled lines to help me write my letters neatly.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A notebook is where you write in school. It has many blank pages.
school_objects.sentence: Write today's date in your notebook.

school_objects.meaning: A folded piece of cardboard or plastic that holds loose papers
school_objects.example: I keep my homework in my red folder so I don't lose it on the way to school.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A folder holds your papers together so they don't get lost.
school_objects.sentence: Put your worksheet inside your folder.

school_objects.meaning: A hard cover with metal rings that hold hole-punched paper
school_objects.example: My three-ring binder has dividers for math, reading, science, and social studies.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A binder is like a big folder with rings that hold the paper in place.
school_objects.sentence: Open the rings to add more paper to your binder.

school_objects.meaning: A tool that makes round holes in paper so it can go in a binder
school_objects.example: I use the hole punch to make three holes in my paper for my binder.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A hole punch makes holes in paper so you can put it in a binder.
school_objects.sentence: Line up the paper carefully before you punch the holes.

school_objects.meaning: A container that holds your lunch food to take to school
school_objects.example: My metal lunchbox has a dinosaur on it and keeps my sandwich cool until noon.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A lunchbox carries your food to school so you can eat at lunchtime.
school_objects.sentence: Don't forget to put your lunchbox in your backpack.

school_objects.meaning: A reusable container for carrying drinking water
school_objects.example: I fill my blue water bottle at the fountain when I am thirsty during recess.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A water bottle holds your water so you can stay hydrated at school.
school_objects.sentence: Please fill your water bottle before we go outside.

school_objects.meaning: A small electronic machine that performs math calculations
school_objects.example: I use my calculator to check if 5 plus 3 equals 8 and 12 minus 4 equals 8.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A calculator helps you do math faster. It has number buttons and a screen.
school_objects.sentence: Use your calculator to solve the big multiplication problem.

school_objects.meaning: A round model of the Earth showing continents, countries, and oceans
school_objects.example: The globe spins around so I can see all the countries and find where I live.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A globe is a round map of the whole world that you can spin.
school_objects.sentence: Spin the globe and point to Africa.

school_objects.meaning: A drawing of an area showing where places are located
school_objects.example: The map of our town shows my house, my school, the park, and the library.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A map helps you find where places are. It's like a picture of the world.
school_objects.sentence: Look at the map to find the treasure.

school_objects.meaning: A tool that shows which direction is north, south, east, and west
school_objects.example: The compass needle always points north, so we can find our way in the forest.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A compass tells you which way is north so you don't get lost.
school_objects.sentence: Follow the compass north to reach the campsite.

school_objects.meaning: A device that tells what time it is, usually with numbers and hands
school_objects.example: The clock on the wall says 12 o'clock, so it is time to go to lunch.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A clock tells you what time it is so you know when to do things.
school_objects.sentence: When the big hand is on 12 and the little hand is on 3, it is 3 o'clock.

school_objects.meaning: A chart showing the days, weeks, and months of the year
school_objects.example: We look at the calendar every morning to see which day it is and what we will learn.
school_objects.kid_friendly_explanation: A calendar shows the days of the week and months of the year.
school_objects.sentence: Circle your birthday on the calendar.
